name: Harold Vick
abstract: Harold Vick (April 3, 1936 – November 13, 1987) was an American jazz saxophonist and flautist.

concerts.label: Kool Jazz Festival: A Tribute to Wes Montgomery

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: Friends of ours: Jack Gregg, Bob Moses, Harold Vick and Juma Santos... So we though the idea of having a cooperative band… also Harold Vick on saxophone… would be a great idea.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Buddy Tate
about_quotes.evidence: And then all the time though, after Milton, I was listening to a tenor player, me and Herschel, also Milton, by the name of Prince Robinson. That's Harold Vick's uncle.

about_quotes.speaker: Barry Harris
about_quotes.evidence: I think the guest soloists were Slide Hamilton, Jordan – Clifford Jordan, Tommy Turrentine, who could be the fourth? Maybe Harold Vick, something like that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Barry Harris — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
